rxn_class_summary: Telescoped electrocatalytic coupling of aromatic alkenes with NH3 and CO2 (or CS2) via an iodide-mediated N-H aziridination followed by CO2/CS2 ring expansion to give 2-oxazolidinones (or 2-thiazolidinethiones).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide generates an electrophilic NH2I species (from NH3) that mediates N-H aziridination of the styrene; H2 is evolved at the cathode. Iodide is regenerated and reused in the subsequent (non-electrochemical) ring expansion step.

key_intermediates.name: zwitterionic aziridinium carbamate adduct (aziridine N + CO2)
ring-opened iodo-carbamate (iodide attack at benzylic carbon)

electron_flow: At the anode, iodide is oxidized and combines with NH3 to give NH2I, which adds across the styrene to form a vicinal iodoamine; base-assisted cyclization releases iodide and gives the N-H aziridine, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode. In the second (thermal) cycle, the aziridine nitrogen attacks CO2 to form a carbamate zwitterion; iodide opens the ring regioselectively at the benzylic carbon, and ring closure expels iodide to deliver the 2-oxazolidinone, regenerating the iodide mediator.

probe_reagents.name: LiI (variable loading: 0, 0.2, 2 equiv)
probe_reagents.observation: CO2 insertion yield drops from 92% (2 equiv LiI) to 9% (no halide) despite full aziridine consumption; higher LiI suppresses oligomerization and improves regioselectivity (2a:3a up to 18:1).

probe_reagents.name: LiBr
probe_reagents.observation: Similar trend to LiI but less pronounced, confirming halide mediator role; iodide is the better halide.

probe_reagents.name: trans-beta-methylstyrene
probe_reagents.observation: Gives cis/trans product mixture (59:41 cis-major), indicating loss of stereochemistry consistent with an SN1-like ring-opening at the benzylic carbon.

probe_reagents.name: Telescoped vs. salt-removed control
probe_reagents.observation: Removing salts after aziridination and re-adding controlled LiI amounts shows iodide is required for efficient ring expansion (double use of catalyst confirmed).

notes: Regioselectivity for the 5-aryl isomer is rationalized by benzylic-cation stabilization favoring iodide attack at the more hindered benzylic carbon (SN1-like), supported by partial racemization with trans-beta-methylstyrene. An analogous mechanism is proposed for CS2 to give 2-thiazolidinethiones. Parasitic pathways (oligomerization/copolymerization of aziridine with CO2) compete when iodide is low.
